- fix(ux): prevent false 'session expired' message on first visit

    ```
    The session expired popup was confusing new visitors who never logged in.
    This happened because handleSessionExpired() didn't check if a session
    actually existed before showing the expiration message.

    Now we check for both userData AND authToken in localStorage:
    - If neither exists → user was never logged in, ignore 401 silently
    - If either exists → user HAD a session, show the expiration message

    Tested: Fresh visit no longer shows popup. Actual logout still works.
    ```
- fix(security): protect root URL upload form with authentication
    The root route '/' was serving a CV upload form without any authentication,
    allowing anyone to access the upload interface. This could potentially be
    exploited to upload malicious files or spam the system.
    Added @login_required decorator to the index() route so that:

    - Unauthenticated users get redirected to the login page
    - Only logged-in users can access the CV upload form
    - Consistent with how other sensitive endpoints are protected

    Tested: Visiting api.tovtech.org/ while logged out now redirects to login."
- feat(security): add rate limiting to protect against brute force and DoS
    Implemented Flask-Limiter to protect all API endpoints from abuse:
    Global protection:

    - All endpoints: 100 requests per minute (prevents DoS)

    Stricter limits on sensitive auth endpoints:

    - /login: 5 requests per minute (stops password guessing attacks)
    - /register: 3 requests per minute (prevents spam account creation)
    - /reset_password: 3 requests per minute (prevents email flooding)

    Technical implementation:

    - Added Flask-Limiter 3.5.1 to requirements.txt
    - Created global limiter instance in config/globals.py
    - Initialized limiter in app.py
    - Applied @limiter.limit decorators to auth.py endpoints

    When limit exceeded, returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests.
    Uses in-memory storage with fixed-window strategy."
- fix(perf): prevent duplicate /me API calls on page load
    The AuthContext was making two identical requests to /me endpoint on every
    page load. This happened because React StrictMode (enabled in development)
    intentionally runs useEffect hooks twice to help catch bugs.
    Added a useRef-based guard to ensure the session fetch only happens once:

    - Created hasFetchedSession ref initialized to false
    - useEffect checks this ref and returns early if already true
    - Set ref to true before initiating the fetch

    This reduces unnecessary network traffic and eliminates the duplicate 401
    errors that were showing in the console for unauthenticated users.
    Tested: Network tab now shows single /me request instead of two."

- The Problem:
    Windows Explorer caches disk space values. After running cleanup scripts, Explorer shows stale (old) free space numbers until you restart it
    or reboot.
    The Fix:

    1. Added refresh-disk function that:
        - Kills and restarts explorer.exe (forces cache flush)
        - Clears icon cache files
        - Runs garbage collection to flush filesystem buffers
        - Shows accurate disk space via WMI (not cached PowerShell drives)
    2. Updated ccc1 from:
        function ccc1 { siz; mgr; ccsizes; cleanc; siz }
    3. To:
        function ccc1 { siz; mgr; ccsizes; cleanc; refresh-disk }
